modes.title: Modes
modes.body:
Working Modes (top bar)

[Edit Measure]
- The large Edit Measure button opens Edit Measure (MIDI) for the current selection
- It is a piano-roll style editor for note timing, duration, pitch, and velocity inside the selected measures
- Velocity, AI Voicing, AI Accomp, and playback are available directly inside the dialog

[Measure Select]
- Left click selects the clicked measure
- Left drag selects a measure range
- Playback ranges and many edit commands use the current measure selection

[Note Select]
- Left click on a note selects that note
- Left drag selects notes in the rectangle
- Shift+click adds to the current selection

[Score Edit]
- Left click on empty score space applies note/rest input tools
- Accidental and note eraser tools still apply directly on note click
- Pressing a pinned top-bar tool automatically switches the app into Score Edit mode

[Status Bar]
- The bar below the top menu shows the current mode, tool, track, selected measures, rhythm, melody, and AI state

tools.title: Tools
tools.body:
Input Tools

[Left Palette Input]
- Notes, rests, erasers, and accidentals are grouped in one section
- Notes are the main grid, rests are shown as an auxiliary row

[Pinned Top-Bar Tools]
- Note length, erase note, and erase measure tools can stay pinned in the top bar
- Pressing a pinned tool automatically switches the app into Score Edit mode

[Note]  Whole / Half / Quarter / 8th / 16th / 32nd / 64th
- Each duration has a dotted variant (adds 50%)
- Click on score to insert at that pitch and beat
- Notes at the same beat stack into chords

[Rest]
- Insert a rest directly when needed
- Most rest cleanup is still handled automatically by normalization

[Eraser]
- Erase Measure: clear the clicked measure while keeping the measure slot
- Erase Note: delete only the clicked note

[Accidental]  Flat(b) / Natural / Sharp(#)

[Edit Measure (MIDI)]
- Edit / Insert / Delete / Tie modes are available for precise note editing inside the measure
- Velocity opens a popup editor for the selected note range
- The measure navigation slider and playback button help you review edits quickly

[Score Setup]
- Time Signature: 4/4, 3/4, 2/4, 6/8, 12/8
- Key Signature: Major/Minor, 3 apply modes
- Clef: Treble / Bass (entire track)
- Expression Marks: accel., rit., p, mf, f, cresc., dim., Ped., *

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
shortcuts.body:
Keyboard Shortcuts

[Edit]
Ctrl+Z Undo | Ctrl+Y Redo
Delete selected | Ctrl+C Copy | Ctrl+V Paste

[Selection]
Escape Clear

[Transpose / Move]
Up/Down +/-1 semitone
Ctrl+Up/Down Diatonic step
Left/Right Move notes

[View]
Mouse wheel Scroll
Ctrl+wheel Zoom (0.5x~1.6x)

selection.title: Selection
selection.body:
Selection Details

[Measure Select]
- Left click/drag selects measures only in Measure Select mode
- Shift+click adds to measure selection
- Clear Selection keeps measure slots
- Delete removes selected measures

[Note Select]
- Left click/drag selects notes only in Note Select mode
- Shift+click adds notes to selection
- Delete removes selected notes

[Score Edit and Selection]
- In Score Edit mode, applying the active input tool takes priority over selection
- With a pinned top-bar tool active, clicks insert or erase immediately

[Paste Mode]
- Ctrl+V starts paste preview
- Left click applies paste
- Esc exits paste mode

playback.title: Playback
playback.body:
Playback

[Player Controls]
- Play / Pause / Stop
- Play mode toggles: selected range only / selected range to end / whole song
- Position slider seek
- Volume control

[Behavior]
- Selected range only: plays only selected measures
- Selected range to end: starts at the first selected measure and keeps the original full song timeline
- If a range mode has no selected measure, playback falls back to the last worked note's measure
- Whole song: always plays from the beginning to the end
- Hidden tracks are excluded

[Audio Export]
- WAV: lossless
- MP3: compressed (requires lame.exe)
- MIDI: standard .mid export

track.title: Track
track.body:
Track Management

[Track Add / Delete]
- Add creates a new track
- Delete opens a checklist popup so multiple tracks can be deleted at once

[Hide / Show]
- Hide removes track from view/playback
- Show restores hidden tracks

[Track Tools]
- Merge / Split by pitch / Move track order are available from the Track section

[Track Info]
- Click track header to edit instrument
- Program / Bank / Percussion / Volume / Pan

[Lyrics]
- Double-click measure lyric
- Use Lyrics View/Edit for bulk edit

compose.title: Compose
compose.body:
Compose Assist

[Top Menu]
- Open composition features from the Rhythm, Melody, Helper, and AI Helper menus
- The current rhythm and melody state are visible in the top bar and status bar

[Rhythm]
- Reassigns rhythm patterns to the selected measures
- Genre-based patterns and re-apply actions are available

[Melody]
- Applies melody transforms such as Inversion
- Works from the selected notes or selected measures

[Helper]
- Opens chord suggestion, voice leading, regular accompaniment, and composition guide tools
- Compose overlay labels such as Note Guide and Chord Suggestion follow the current UI language
- Voice Leading exposes detailed tuning parameters in its own panel

[AI Helper]
- Opens AI Voicing and AI accompaniment tools
- Builtin ONNX and TinyGRU models can be selected for generation

[AI inside Edit Measure (MIDI)]
- AI Voicing reorganizes or regenerates voicing for the selected measures on the current track
- AI Accomp generates accompaniment to fit the current melody and harmony
- AI accompaniment styles include AI Chord Beat, AI Arpeggio, AI Free, AI Free (Melody Variation), and AI Drum Beat

files.title: Files
files.body:
File Management

[Commands] New / Open / Save / Save As / Settings
[Multi-Document Tabs] Multiple scores in tabs
[Export] Score: PDF/PNG/JPG | Audio: WAV/MP3/MIDI
[Format] JSON (.json / .arkscore)
[Settings] File > Settings edits AppData-based options, including UI language
[Troubleshooting] Check SoundFont, track visibility, lame.exe

leftdock.title: Left Dock
leftdock.body:
Left Dock & Palette

[Layout]
- Top: Tool palette
- Bottom: Player controls
- Collapse button minimizes dock

[Tool Categories]
- File / Edit / Score Setup / Input / Track / Compose Assist
- Input groups notes, rests, erasers, and accidentals together
- Rests are shown as a secondary row under the main note tools
- File > About shows app info and author contact

[Top-Bar Integration]
- Frequently used input tools can stay pinned in the top bar for quick access
- The top bar is grouped into undo/redo, Edit Measure, selection modes, pinned tools, and compose menus
- The status bar below it keeps the current working state visible
